name: Filter-value-hallucination probe (iter 3 — prompt clause)
description: Tests the new FILTER VALUES MUST BE LITERAL DATA prompt clause against the 2 baseline-190 failures where the LLM emitted descriptive strings or column references as filter values. Two control cases verify no regression on the label-fix (iter 1) and the self-join drop (iter 2).

Scenarios.Question: SLA breaches in the last quarter by department
Scenarios.Notes: From B-DOM-012. Previously emitted '[ServiceTypes.Id...]' as a filter value → nvarchar→int conversion failed. With the prompt clause, the LLM should drop or correctly literal the filter.

Scenarios.Question: active customers in the West region with at least one payment
Scenarios.Notes: From B-FLT-022. Previously emitted 'West Region Ids' as a filter value. With the clause, the LLM should join to Regions and filter NameEn='West' (or drop the region filter).

Scenarios.Question: top 5 customers by total bill amount
Scenarios.Notes: Control: iter 1 label fix should still produce Customers.FullNameEn + SUM.

Scenarios.Question: list every region with its parent region
Scenarios.Notes: Control: iter 2 should still drop the unresolved ParentRegion.NameEn and execute.
